articles.text:
[32011R1169] CHAPTER IV — MANDATORY FOOD INFORMATION SECTION 2 — Detailed provisions on mandatory particulars Article 21 — Labelling of certain substances or products causing allergies or intolerances (part 2/2)

Where, in the case of the emergence of a risk to consumers’ health, imperative grounds of urgency so require, the procedure provided for in Article 52 shall apply to delegated acts adopted pursuant to this Article.

metadata.article_title: Labelling of certain substances or products causing allergies or intolerances
metadata.section: SECTION 2 — Detailed provisions on mandatory particulars

articles.text:
[32011R1169] CHAPTER IV — MANDATORY FOOD INFORMATION SECTION 2 — Detailed provisions on mandatory particulars Article 21 — Labelling of certain substances or products causing allergies or intolerances (part 1/2)

Without prejudice to the rules adopted under Article 44(2), the particulars referred to in point (c) of Article 9(1) shall meet the following requirements:
(a)they shall be indicated in the list of ingredients in accordance with the rules laid down in Article 18(1), with a clear reference to the name of the substance or product as listed in Annex II; and
(a)
they shall be indicated in the list of ingredients in accordance with the rules laid down in Article 18(1), with a clear reference to the name of the substance or product as listed in Annex II; and
(b)the name of the substance or product as listed in Annex II shall be emphasised through a typeset that clearly distinguishes it from the rest of the list of ingredients, for example by means of the font, style or background colour.
(b)
the name of the substance or product as listed in Annex II shall be emphasised through a typeset that clearly distinguishes it from the rest of the list of ingredients, for example by means of the font, style or background colour.
In the absence of a list of ingredients, the indication of the particulars referred to in point (c) of Article 9(1) shall comprise the word ‘contains’ followed by the name of the substance or product as listed in Annex II.
Where several ingredients or processing aids of a food originate from a single substance or product listed in Annex II, the labelling shall make it clear for each ingredient or processing aid concerned.
The indication of the particulars referred to in point (c) of Article 9(1) shall not be required in cases where the name of the food clearly refers to the substance or product concerned.
In order to ensure better information for consumers and to take account of the most recent scientific progress and technical knowledge, the Commission shall systematically re-examine and, where necessary, update the list in Annex II by means of delegated acts, in accordance with Article 51.

metadata.article_title: Labelling of certain substances or products causing allergies or intolerances
metadata.section: SECTION 2 — Detailed provisions on mandatory particulars

articles.text:
[32011R1169] CHAPTER IV — MANDATORY FOOD INFORMATION SECTION 1 — Content and presentation Article 16 — Omission of certain mandatory particulars

In the case of glass bottles intended for reuse which are indelibly marked and which therefore bear no label, ring or collar only the particulars listed in points (a), (c), (e), (f) and (l) of Article 9(1) shall be mandatory.
In the case of packaging or containers the largest surface of which has an area of less than 10 cm2only the particulars listed in points (a), (c), (e) and (f) of Article 9(1) shall be mandatory on the package or on the label. The particulars referred to in point (b) of Article 9(1) shall be provided through other means or shall be made available at the request of the consumer.
Without prejudice to other Union provisions requiring a mandatory nutrition declaration, the declaration referred to in point (l) of Article 9(1) shall not be mandatory for the foods listed in Annex V.
Without prejudice to other Union provisions requiring a list of ingredients or a mandatory nutrition declaration, the particulars referred to in points (b) and (l) of Article 9(1) shall not be mandatory for beverages containing more than 1,2 % by volume of alcohol.
By 13 December 2014, the Commission shall produce a report concerning the application of Article 18 and Article 30(1) to the products referred to in this paragraph, and addressing whether alcoholic beverages should in future be covered, in particular, by the requirement to provide the information on the energy value, and the reasons justifying possible exemptions, taking into account the need to ensure coherence with other relevant Union policies. In this context, the Commission shall consider the need to propose a definition of ‘alcopops’.
The Commission shall accompany that report by a legislative proposal, if appropriate, determining the rules for a list of ingredients or a mandatory nutrition declaration for those products.

[32011R1169] CHAPTER V — VOLUNTARY FOOD INFORMATION Article 36 — Applicable requirements (part 1/2)

Where food information referred to in Articles 9 and 10 is provided on a voluntary basis, such information shall comply with the requirements laid down in Sections 2 and 3 of Chapter IV.
Food information provided on a voluntary basis shall meet the following requirements:
(a)it shall not mislead the consumer, as referred to in Article 7;
(a)
it shall not mislead the consumer, as referred to in Article 7;
(b)it shall not be ambiguous or confusing for the consumer; and
(b)
it shall not be ambiguous or confusing for the consumer; and
(c)it shall, where appropriate, be based on the relevant scientific data.
(c)
it shall, where appropriate, be based on the relevant scientific data.
The Commission shall adopt implementing acts on the application of the requirements referred to in paragraph 2 of this Article to the following voluntary food information:
(a)information on the possible and unintentional presence in food of substances or products causing allergies or intolerances;
(a)
information on the possible and unintentional presence in food of substances or products causing allergies or intolerances;
(b)information related to suitability of a food for vegetarians or vegans;
(b)
information related to suitability of a food for vegetarians or vegans;
(c)the indication of reference intakes for specific population groups in addition to the reference intakes set out in Annex XIII; and
(c)
the indication of reference intakes for specific population groups in addition to the reference intakes set out in Annex XIII; and
(d)information on the absence or reduced presence of gluten in food.
(d)
information on the absence or reduced presence of gluten in food.
Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 48(2).

[32011R1169] CHAPTER VII — IMPLEMENTING, AMENDING AND FINAL PROVISIONS Article 49 — Amendments to Regulation (EC) No 1924/2006

The first and second paragraphs of Article 7 of Regulation (EC) No 1924/2006 are replaced by the following:
‘Nutrition labelling of products on which a nutrition and/or health claim is made shall be mandatory, with the exception of generic advertising. The information to be provided shall consist of that specified in Article 30(1) of Regulation (EU) No 1169/2011 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 25 October 2011 on the provision of food information to consumers (*1). Where a nutrition and/or health claim is made for a nutrient referred to in Article 30(2) of Regulation (EU) No 1169/2011 the amount of that nutrient shall be declared in accordance with Articles 31 to 34 of that Regulation.
The amount(s) of the substance(s) to which a nutrition or health claim relates that does not appear in the nutrition labelling shall be stated in the same field of vision as the nutrition labelling and be expressed in accordance with Articles 31, 32 and 33 of Regulation (EU) No 1169/2011. The units of measurement used to express the amount of the substance shall be appropriate for the individual substances concerned.

metadata.chapter: CHAPTER VII — IMPLEMENTING, AMENDING AND FINAL PROVISIONS

articles.text:
[32011R1169] CHAPTER IV — MANDATORY FOOD INFORMATION SECTION 2 — Detailed provisions on mandatory particulars Article 22 — Quantitative indication of ingredients

The indication of the quantity of an ingredient or category of ingredients used in the manufacture or preparation of a food shall be required where the ingredient or category of ingredients concerned:
(a)appears in the name of the food or is usually associated with that name by the consumer;
(a)
appears in the name of the food or is usually associated with that name by the consumer;
(b)is emphasised on the labelling in words, pictures or graphics; or
(b)
is emphasised on the labelling in words, pictures or graphics; or
(c)is essential to characterise a food and to distinguish it from products with which it might be confused because of its name or appearance.
(c)
is essential to characterise a food and to distinguish it from products with which it might be confused because of its name or appearance.
Technical rules for applying paragraph 1, including specific cases where the quantitative indication shall not be required in respect of certain ingredients, are laid down in Annex VIII.

metadata.section: SECTION 2 — Detailed provisions on mandatory particulars

articles.text:
[32011R1169] CHAPTER IV — MANDATORY FOOD INFORMATION SECTION 1 — Content and presentation Article 12 — Availability and placement of mandatory food information

Mandatory food information shall be available and shall be easily accessible, in accordance with this Regulation, for all foods.
In the case of prepacked food, mandatory food information shall appear directly on the package or on a label attached thereto.
In order to ensure that consumers benefit from other means of provision of mandatory food information better adapted for certain mandatory particulars, and provided that the same level of information as by means of the package or the label is ensured, the Commission, taking into account evidence of uniform consumer understanding and of the wide use of these means by consumers, may establish, by means of delegated acts in accordance with Article 51, criteria subject to which certain mandatory particulars may be expressed by means other than on the package or on the label.
For the purposes of ensuring the uniform implementation of paragraph 3 of this Article, the Commission may adopt implementing acts on the modalities of application of the criteria referred to in paragraph 3 in order to express certain mandatory particulars by means other than on the package or on the label. Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 48(2).
In the case of non-prepacked food, the provisions of Article 44 shall apply.

metadata.article_title: Availability and placement of mandatory food information
General labelling requirements for food additives not intended for sale to the final consumer

articles.text:
[32011R1169] CHAPTER I — GENERAL PROVISIONS Article 1 — Subject matter and scope

This Regulation provides the basis for the assurance of a high level of consumer protection in relation to food information, taking into account the differences in the perception of consumers and their information needs whilst ensuring the smooth functioning of the internal market.
This Regulation establishes the general principles, requirements and responsibilities governing food information, and in particular food labelling. It lays down the means to guarantee the right of consumers to information and procedures for the provision of food information, taking into account the need to provide sufficient flexibility to respond to future developments and new information requirements.
This Regulation shall apply to food business operators at all stages of the food chain, where their activities concern the provision of food information to consumers. It shall apply to all foods intended for the final consumer, including foods delivered by mass caterers, and foods intended for supply to mass caterers.
This Regulation shall apply to catering services provided by transport undertakings when the departure takes place on the territories of the Member States to which the Treaties apply.
This Regulation shall apply without prejudice to labelling requirements provided for in specific Union provisions applicable to particular foods.

[32008R1333] CHAPTER IV — LABELLING Article 24 — Labelling requirement for foods containing certain food colours

Without prejudice to Directive 2000/13/EC, the labelling of food containing the food colours listed in Annex V to this Regulation shall include the additional information set out in that Annex.
In relation to the information provided in paragraph 1 of this Article, Article 13(2) of Directive 2000/13/EC shall apply accordingly.
Where necessary as a result of scientific progress or technical development, Annex V shall be amended by measures, designed to amend non-essential elements of this Regulation, in accordance with the regulatory procedure with scrutiny referred to in Article 28(4).

metadata.article_title: Labelling requirement for foods containing certain food colours

articles.text:
[32006R1924] CHAPTER II GENERAL PRINCIPLES Article 7 — Nutrition information

The obligation and the modalities for providing information pursuant to Directive 90/496/EEC where a nutrition claim is made shall apply,mutatis mutandis, where a health claim is made, with the exception of generic advertising. However, the information to be provided shall consist of information in Group 2 as defined in Article 4(1) of Directive 90/496/EEC.
In addition and as the case may be, the amount(s) of the substance(s) to which a nutrition or health claim relates that does not appear in the nutrition labelling shall also be stated in the same field of vision of the nutrition information and be expressed in accordance with Article 6 of Directive 90/496/EEC.
In the case of food supplements, the nutrition information shall be provided in accordance with Article 8 of Directive 2002/46/EC.

[32006R1924] CHAPTER I SUBJECT MATTER, SCOPE AND DEFINITIONS Article 2 — Definitions (part 1/2)

1.   For the purposes of this Regulation:
(a)
the definitions of ‘food’, ‘food business operator’, ‘placing on the market’ and ‘final consumer’, set out in Articles 2, 3(3), 3(8) and 3(18) of Regulation (EC) No 178/2002 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 28 January 2002 laying down the general principles and requirements of food law, establishing the European Food Safety Authority and laying down procedures in matters of food safety(14), shall apply;
(b)
the definition of ‘food supplement’ set out in Directive 2002/46/EC shall apply;
(c)
the definitions of ‘nutrition labelling’, ‘protein’, ‘carbohydrate’, ‘sugars’, ‘fat’, ‘saturates’, ‘mono-unsaturates’, ‘poly-unsaturates’ and ‘fibre’ set out in Directive 90/496/EEC shall apply;
(d)
the definition of ‘labelling’ set out in Article 1(3)(a) of Directive 2000/13/EC shall apply.
2.   The following definitions shall also apply:
1.
‘claim’ means any message or representation, which is not mandatory under Community or national legislation, including pictorial, graphic or symbolic representation, in any form, which states, suggests or implies that a food has particular characteristics;
2.
‘nutrient’ means protein, carbohydrate, fat, fibre, sodium, vitamins and minerals listed in the Annex to Directive 90/496/EEC, and substances which belong to or are components of one of those categories;
3.
‘other substance’ means a substance other than a nutrient that has a nutritional or physiological effect;
4.
‘nutrition claim’ means any claim which states, suggests or implies that a food has particular beneficial nutritional properties due to:
(a)
the energy (calorific value) it
(i)
provides,
(ii)
provides at a reduced or increased rate, or
(iii)
does not provide; and/or
(b)
the nutrients or other substances it
(i)
contains,
(ii)
contains in reduced or increased proportions, or
(iii)
does not contain;
5.

[32002R0178] CHAPTER II — GENERAL FOOD LAW SECTION 4 — GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FOOD LAW Article 16 — Presentation

Without prejudice to more specific provisions of food law, the labelling, advertising and presentation of food or feed, including their shape, appearance or packaging, the packaging materials used, the manner in which they are arranged and the setting in which they are displayed, and the information which is made available about them through whatever medium, shall not mislead consumers.

metadata.section: SECTION 4 — GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FOOD LAW

articles.text:
[32008R1332] CHAPTER III LABELLING Article 12 — Labelling of food enzymes and food enzyme preparations intended for sale to the final consumer

1.   Without prejudice to Directive 2000/13/EC, Council Directive 89/396/EEC of 14 June 1989 on indications or marks identifying the lot to which a foodstuff belongs(17)and Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003, food enzymes and food enzyme preparations sold singly or mixed with each other and/or other food ingredients intended for sale to the final consumer may be marketed only if their packaging contains the following information:
(a)
the name laid down under this Regulation in respect of each food enzyme or a sales description which includes the name of each food enzyme or in the absence of such a name, the accepted name laid down in the nomenclature of the IUBMB;
(b)
the statement ‘for food’ or the statement ‘restricted use in food’ or a more specific reference to its intended food use.
2.   For the information provided for in paragraph 1 of this Article, Article 13(2) of Directive 2000/13/EC shall apply accordingly.

metadata.article_title: Labelling of food enzymes and food enzyme preparations intended for sale to the final consumer

articles.text:
[32008R1332] CHAPTER III LABELLING Article 11 — General labelling requirements for food enzymes and food enzyme preparations not intended for sale to the final consumer (part 2/2)

3.   The packaging or containers of food enzyme preparations shall bear a list of all components in descending order of their percentage by weight of the total.
4.   By way of derogation from paragraphs 1, 2 and 3, the information required in paragraph 1 points (e) to (g) and in paragraphs 2 and 3 may appear merely on the documents relating to the consignment which are to be supplied with or prior to the delivery, provided that the indication ‘not for retail sale’ appears on an easily visible part of the packaging or container of the product in question.
5.   By way of derogation from paragraphs 1, 2 and 3, where food enzymes and food enzyme preparations are supplied in tankers all of the information may appear merely on the accompanying documents relating to the consignment which are to be supplied with the delivery.

metadata.article_title: General labelling requirements for food enzymes and food enzyme preparations not intended for sale to the final consumer

articles.text:
[32003R1829] CHAPTER II — GENETICALLY MODIFIED FOOD Section 2 — Labelling Article 13 — Requirements (part 3/3)

implications for the health of certain sections of the population;
(b)where a food may give rise to ethical or religious concerns.
(b)
where a food may give rise to ethical or religious concerns.
In addition to the labelling requirements referred to in paragraph 1 and as specified in the authorisation, the labelling of foods falling within the scope of this Section which do not have a conventional counterpart shall contain appropriate information about the nature and the characteristics of the foods concerned.

metadata.article_title: General labelling requirements for flavourings not intended for sale to the final consumer

articles.text:
[32002R0178] CHAPTER II — GENERAL FOOD LAW SECTION 4 — GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FOOD LAW Article 14 — Food safety requirements (part 1/2)

Food shall not be placed on the market if it is unsafe.
Food shall be deemed to be unsafe if it is considered to be:
(a)injurious to health;
(a)
injurious to health;
(b)unfit for human consumption.
(b)
unfit for human consumption.
In determining whether any food is unsafe, regard shall be had:
(a)to the normal conditions of use of the food by the consumer and at each stage of production, processing and distribution, and
(a)
to the normal conditions of use of the food by the consumer and at each stage of production, processing and distribution, and
(b)to the information provided to the consumer, including information on the label, or other information generally available to the consumer concerning the avoidance of specific adverse health effects from a particular food or category of foods.
(b)
to the information provided to the consumer, including information on the label, or other information generally available to the consumer concerning the avoidance of specific adverse health effects from a particular food or category of foods.
In determining whether any food is injurious to health, regard shall be had:
(a)not only to the probable immediate and/or short-term and/or long-term effects of that food on the health of a person consuming it, but also on subsequent generations;
(a)
not only to the probable immediate and/or short-term and/or long-term effects of that food on the health of a person consuming it, but also on subsequent generations;
(b)to the probable cumulative toxic effects;
(b)
to the probable cumulative toxic effects;
(c)to the particular health sensitivities of a specific category of consumers where the food is intended for that category of consumers.
(c)
to the particular health sensitivities of a specific category of consumers where the food is intended for that category of consumers.

metadata.section: SECTION 4 — GENERAL REQUIREMENTS OF FOOD LAW

articles.text:
[32006R1925] CHAPTER II — ADDITION OF VITAMINS AND MINERALS Article 7 — Labelling, presentation and advertising

The labelling, presentation and advertising of foods to which vitamins and minerals have been added shall not include any mention stating or implying that a balanced and varied diet cannot provide appropriate quantities of nutrients. Where appropriate, a derogation concerning a specific nutrient and designed to amend non-essential elements of this Regulation by supplementing it may be adopted in accordance with the regulatory procedure with scrutiny referred to in Article 14(3).
The labelling, presentation and advertising of foods to which vitamins and minerals have been added shall not mislead or deceive the consumer as to the nutritional merit of a food that may result from the addition of these nutrients.
Nutrition labelling of products to which vitamins and minerals have been added and which are covered by this Regulation shall be compulsory. The information to be provided shall consist of that specified in Article 30(1) of Regulation (EU) No 1169/2011 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 25 October 2011 on the provision of food information to consumers (3) and of the total amounts present of the vitamins and minerals when added to the food.
The labelling of products to which vitamins and minerals have been added may bear a statement indicating such addition under the conditions laid down in Regulation (EC) No 1924/2006.
This Article shall apply without prejudice to other provisions of food law applicable to specified categories of foods.
Rules for implementing this Article may be specified in accordance with the procedure referred to in Article 14(2).

metadata.chapter: CHAPTER II — ADDITION OF VITAMINS AND MINERALS

articles.text:
[32008R1334] CHAPTER IV — LABELLING Article 18 — Other labelling requirements

Articles 14 to 17 shall be without prejudice to more detailed or more extensive laws, regulations or administrative provisions regarding weights and measures or applying to the presentation, classification, packaging and labelling of dangerous substances and preparations or applying to the transport of such substances and preparations.

[32006R1925] CHAPTER IV — GENERAL AND FINAL PROVISIONS Article 15 — Monitoring

To facilitate efficient monitoring of foods to which vitamins and minerals have been added, and of foods containing substances listed in Annex III, Parts B and C, Member States may require the manufacturer or the person placing such foods on the market in their territory to notify the competent authority of that placing on the market by providing a model of the label used for the product. In such cases, information on the withdrawal of the product from the market may also be required.

metadata.chapter: CHAPTER IV — GENERAL AND FINAL PROVISIONS

metadata.article_title: Notification of a traditional food from a third country
metadata.section: Specific rules for traditional foods from third countries

articles.text:
[32015R2283] CHAPTER IV ADDITIONAL PROCEDURAL RULES AND OTHER REQUIREMENTS Article 25 — Additional information requirements

Any food business operator which has placed a novel food on the market shall immediately inform the Commission of any information of which it has become aware concerning:
(a)
any new scientific or technical information which might influence the evaluation of the safety of use of the novel food;
(b)
any prohibition or restriction imposed by a third country in which the novel food is placed on the market.
The Commission shall make that information available to the Member States.

metadata.section: ADDITIONAL PROCEDURAL RULES AND OTHER REQUIREMENTS

articles.text:
[32004R0853] CHAPTER II — FOOD BUSINESS OPERATORS' OBLIGATIONS Article 5 — Health and identification marking

Food business operators shall not place on the market a product of animal origin handled in an establishment subject to approval in accordance with Article 4(2) unless it has either:
(a)a health mark applied in accordance with Regulation (EC) No 854/2004;or
(a)
a health mark applied in accordance with Regulation (EC) No 854/2004;
or
(b)when that Regulation does not provide for the application of a health mark, an identification mark applied in accordance with Annex II, Section I, of this Regulation.
(b)
when that Regulation does not provide for the application of a health mark, an identification mark applied in accordance with Annex II, Section I, of this Regulation.
Food business operators may apply an identification mark to a product of animal origin only if the product has been manufactured in accordance with this Regulation in establishments meeting the requirements of Article 4.
Food business operators may not remove a health mark applied in accordance with Regulation (EC) No 854/2004 from meat unless they cut or process it or work upon it in another manner.

metadata.chapter: CHAPTER II — FOOD BUSINESS OPERATORS' OBLIGATIONS

articles.text:
[32004R0852] CHAPTER IV — IMPORTS AND EXPORTS Article 10 — Imports

As regards the hygiene of imported food, the relevant requirements of food law referred to in Article 11 of Regulation (EC) No 178/2002 shall include the requirements laid down in Articles 3 to 6 of this Regulation.

[32018R0848] CHAPTER IV — LABELLING Article 31 — Labelling of products and substances used in crop production

Notwithstanding the scope of this Regulation as set out in Article 2(1), products and substances used in plant protection products or as fertilisers, soil conditioners or nutrients that have been authorised in accordance with Articles 9 and 24 may bear a reference indicating that those products or substances have been authorised for use in organic production in accordance with this Regulation.

metadata.article_title: Labelling of products and substances used in crop production

articles.text:
[32003R1830] Article 5 — Traceability requirements for products for food and feed produced from GMOs

When placing products produced from GMOs on the market, operators shall ensure that the following information is transmitted in writing to the operator receiving the product:
(a)an indication of each of the food ingredients which is produced from GMOs;
(a)
an indication of each of the food ingredients which is produced from GMOs;
(b)an indication of each of the feed materials or additives which is produced from GMOs;
(b)
an indication of each of the feed materials or additives which is produced from GMOs;
(c)in the case of products for which no list of ingredients exists, an indication that the product is produced from GMOs.
(c)
in the case of products for which no list of ingredients exists, an indication that the product is produced from GMOs.
Without prejudice to Article 6, operators shall have in place systems and standardised procedures to allow the holding of the information specified in paragraph 1 and the identification, for a period of five years from each transaction, of the operator by whom and to whom the products referred to in paragraph 1 have been made available.
Paragraphs 1 and 2 shall be without prejudice to other specific requirements in Community legislation.
Paragraphs 1, 2 and 3 shall not apply to traces of GMOs in products for food and feed produced from GMOs in a proportion no higher than the thresholds established for those GMOs in accordance with Articles 12, 24 or 47 of Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003, provided that these traces of GMOs are adventitious or technically unavoidable.

metadata.article_title: Traceability requirements for products for food and feed produced from GMOs

articles.text:
[32018R0848] CHAPTER IV — LABELLING Article 32 — Compulsory indications (part 3/3)

(c)the indication of the place where the agricultural raw materials were farmed, in accordance with paragraph 2 of this Article and with Article 33(3).
(c)
the indication of the place where the agricultural raw materials were farmed, in accordance with paragraph 2 of this Article and with Article 33(3).
Those implementing acts shall be adopted in accordance with the examination procedure referred to in Article 55(2).

[32004R0853] CHAPTER II — FOOD BUSINESS OPERATORS' OBLIGATIONS Article 3 — General obligations

Food business operators shall comply with the relevant provisions of Annexes II and III.
Food business operators shall not use any substance other than potable water or, when Regulation (EC) No 852/2004 or this Regulation permits its use, clean water, to remove surface contamination from products of animal origin, unless use of the substance has been approved by the Commission. For that purpose the Commission is empowered to adopt delegated acts in accordance with Article 11a supplementing this Regulation. Food business operators shall also comply with any conditions for use that may be adopted under the same procedure. The use of an approved substance shall not affect the food business operator’s duty to comply with the requirements of this Regulation.

metadata.chapter: CHAPTER II — FOOD BUSINESS OPERATORS' OBLIGATIONS

articles.text:
[32003R1830] Article 4 — Traceability and labelling requirements for products consisting of or containing GMOs (part 2/2)

for pre-packaged products consisting of, or containing GMOs, the words ‘This product contains genetically modified organisms’ or ‘This product contains genetically modified [name of organism(s)]’ appear on a label;
(b)for non-pre-packaged products offered to the final consumer the words ‘This product contains genetically modified organisms’ or ‘This product contains genetically modified [name of organism(s)]’ shall appear on, or in connection with, the display of the product.
(b)
for non-pre-packaged products offered to the final consumer the words ‘This product contains genetically modified organisms’ or ‘This product contains genetically modified [name of organism(s)]’ shall appear on, or in connection with, the display of the product.
This paragraph shall be without prejudice to other specific requirements in Community legislation.
Paragraphs 1 to 6 shall not apply to traces of GMOs in products in a proportion no higher than the thresholds established in accordance with Article 21(2) or (3) of Directive 2001/18/EC and in other specific Community legislation, provided that these traces of GMOs are adventitious or technically unavoidable.
Paragraphs 1 to 6 shall not apply to traces of GMOs in products intended for direct use as food, feed or for processing in a proportion no higher than the thresholds established for those GMOs in accordance with Articles 12, 24 or 47 of Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003, provided that these traces of GMOs are adventitious or technically unavoidable.

metadata.article_title: Traceability and labelling requirements for products consisting of or containing GMOs

articles.text:
[32004R0852] CHAPTER IV — IMPORTS AND EXPORTS Article 11 — Exports

As regards the hygiene of exported or re-exported food, the relevant requirements of food law referred to in Article 12 of Regulation (EC) No 178/2002 shall include the requirements laid down in Articles 3 to 6 of this Regulation.

requirements.article_title: Labelling of certain substances or products causing allergies or intolerances
requirements.requirement_summary: Allergens must be indicated in the list of ingredients with a clear reference to the substance name as listed in Annex II, and the allergen name must be visually emphasised (e.g. bold, different font or background colour) to clearly distinguish it from the rest of the ingredients list.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators placing prepacked food on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies when the food contains ingredients or processing aids listed in Annex II causing allergies or intolerances
requirements.source_text_snippet: the name of the substance or product as listed in Annex II shall be emphasised through a typeset that clearly distinguishes it from the rest of the list of ingredients

requirements.article_title: Labelling of certain substances or products causing allergies or intolerances
requirements.requirement_summary: Where there is no list of ingredients, allergen information must be provided using the word 'contains' followed by the name of the substance or product as listed in Annex II.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators placing food without an ingredients list on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies only when the food has no list of ingredients but contains allergens listed in Annex II
requirements.source_text_snippet: the indication of the particulars referred to in point (c) of Article 9(1) shall comprise the word 'contains' followed by the name of the substance or product as listed in Annex II

requirements.article_title: Labelling of certain substances or products causing allergies or intolerances
requirements.requirement_summary: Where several ingredients or processing aids originate from a single allergen listed in Annex II, the labelling must make this clear for each ingredient or processing aid concerned.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators placing food on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies when multiple ingredients derive from a single Annex II allergen
requirements.source_text_snippet: Where several ingredients or processing aids of a food originate from a single substance or product listed in Annex II, the labelling shall make it clear for each ingredient or processing aid concerned

requirements.requirement_summary: For packaging with a largest surface area less than 10 cm², only the food name, allergens, net quantity, and date of minimum durability/use-by date are mandatory on the label. The list of ingredients must be provided through other means or made available at the consumer's request.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators placing food in small packaging (less than 10 cm²) on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies only to packaging or containers with largest surface area less than 10 cm²
requirements.source_text_snippet: In the case of packaging or containers the largest surface of which has an area of less than 10 cm2 only the particulars listed in points (a), (c), (e) and (f) of Article 9(1) shall be mandatory

requirements.requirement_summary: The mandatory nutrition declaration is not required for foods listed in Annex V of the regulation, without prejudice to other EU provisions that may require it.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators placing foods listed in Annex V on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies only to foods listed in Annex V of Regulation (EU) No 1169/2011
requirements.source_text_snippet: the declaration referred to in point (l) of Article 9(1) shall not be mandatory for the foods listed in Annex V

requirements.requirement_summary: Beverages containing more than 1.2% alcohol by volume are exempt from requiring a list of ingredients and mandatory nutrition declaration.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators placing alcoholic beverages (>1.2% ABV) on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies only to beverages containing more than 1.2% by volume of alcohol
requirements.source_text_snippet: the particulars referred to in points (b) and (l) of Article 9(1) shall not be mandatory for beverages containing more than 1,2 % by volume of alcohol

requirements.requirement_summary: Any voluntary food information provided on labels must comply with the mandatory labelling rules in Sections 2 and 3 of Chapter IV. It must not mislead the consumer, must not be ambiguous or confusing, and where appropriate must be based on relevant scientific data.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators providing voluntary food information on labels
requirements.conditions: Applies when food information covered by Articles 9 and 10 is provided voluntarily
requirements.source_text_snippet: Food information provided on a voluntary basis shall meet the following requirements: (a) it shall not mislead the consumer

requirements.requirement_summary: The quantity of an ingredient or category of ingredients must be indicated on the label when that ingredient appears in the food name, is emphasised on the labelling, or is essential to characterise the food and distinguish it from similar products.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators placing prepacked food on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies when the ingredient appears in the food name, is emphasised on labelling, or is essential to characterise the food
requirements.source_text_snippet: The indication of the quantity of an ingredient or category of ingredients used in the manufacture or preparation of a food shall be required

requirements.article_title: Availability and placement of mandatory food information
requirements.requirement_summary: Mandatory food information must be available and easily accessible for all foods. For prepacked food, it must appear directly on the package or on an attached label.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators placing food on the EU market
requirements.source_text_snippet: Mandatory food information shall be available and shall be easily accessible, in accordance with this Regulation, for all foods

requirements.requirement_summary: Food labels must include all mandatory particulars: food name, list of ingredients, allergen information (Annex II), quantity of certain ingredients, net quantity, date of minimum durability or use-by date, storage/use conditions, FBO name and address, country of origin (where required), instructions for use (where needed), and alcoholic strength for beverages >1.2% ABV.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators placing prepacked food on the EU market
requirements.source_text_snippet: indication of the following particulars shall be mandatory

requirements.article_title: General labelling requirements for food additives not intended for sale to the final consumer
requirements.requirement_summary: Food additives not sold to the final consumer must be labelled with the name/E-number, a 'for food' or 'restricted use in food' statement, special storage/use conditions, batch/lot mark, instructions for use, manufacturer/seller details, and maximum quantity indications enabling compliance with the regulation.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators selling food additives not intended for the final consumer
requirements.conditions: Applies when food additives are sold singly or mixed, not for sale to the final consumer
requirements.source_text_snippet: their packaging or containers shall bear the following information

requirements.requirement_summary: The regulation applies to all food business operators at all stages of the food chain where their activities concern the provision of food information to consumers, covering all foods intended for the final consumer including foods delivered by mass caterers.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators at all stages of the food chain providing food information to consumers
requirements.source_text_snippet: This Regulation shall apply to food business operators at all stages of the food chain, where their activities concern the provision of food information to consumers

requirements.article_title: Labelling requirement for foods containing certain food colours
requirements.requirement_summary: Foods containing food colours listed in Annex V of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008 must include the additional labelling information specified in that Annex (warning about effects on children's activity and attention).
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators placing foods containing Annex V food colours on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies only when the food contains food colours listed in Annex V of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008
requirements.source_text_snippet: the labelling of food containing the food colours listed in Annex V to this Regulation shall include the additional information set out in that Annex

requirements.requirement_summary: When a nutrition or health claim is made on a food product, mandatory nutrition labelling is required. The information must consist of that specified in Article 30(1) of Regulation (EU) No 1169/2011. If a claim relates to a nutrient in Article 30(2), its amount must be declared per Articles 31-34.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators making nutrition or health claims on food products
requirements.conditions: Applies only when a nutrition and/or health claim is made on the product
requirements.source_text_snippet: Nutrition labelling of products on which a nutrition and/or health claim is made shall be mandatory

requirements.requirement_summary: The labelling, advertising, and presentation of food (including shape, appearance, packaging, packaging materials, arrangement, display setting, and information provided through any medium) must not mislead consumers.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators involved in labelling, advertising, or presenting food
requirements.source_text_snippet: the labelling, advertising and presentation of food or feed... shall not mislead consumers

requirements.article_title: Labelling of food enzymes and food enzyme preparations intended for sale to the final consumer
requirements.requirement_summary: Food enzymes and food enzyme preparations sold to the final consumer must be labelled with the name of each food enzyme (or the IUBMB accepted name if no regulatory name exists) and a statement indicating 'for food', 'restricted use in food', or a more specific reference to its intended food use.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators selling food enzymes or food enzyme preparations to the final consumer
requirements.conditions: Applies only to food enzymes and food enzyme preparations intended for sale to the final consumer
requirements.source_text_snippet: food enzymes and food enzyme preparations sold singly or mixed with each other and/or other food ingredients intended for sale to the final consumer may be marketed only if their packaging contains the following information

requirements.requirement_summary: Foods containing or produced from GMOs must be labelled with GMO-related information. Where the food differs from its conventional counterpart in composition, nutritional value, intended use, or health implications, the labelling must mention those characteristics as specified in the authorisation.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators placing GM foods on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies when the food falls within the scope of Chapter II of Regulation (EC) No 1829/2003 (contains or is produced from GMOs)
requirements.source_text_snippet: the labelling shall also mention any characteristic or property, as specified in the authorisation

requirements.requirement_summary: For non-prepacked GM food or GM food in small containers (<10 cm²), the required GMO information must be permanently and visibly displayed either on the food display, immediately next to it, or on the packaging material in a sufficiently large font.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators selling non-prepacked GM food or GM food in small containers to the final consumer
requirements.conditions: Applies to non-prepacked GM food or prepacked GM food in containers with largest surface area less than 10 cm²
requirements.source_text_snippet: where the food is offered for sale to the final consumer as non-pre-packaged food, or as pre-packaged food in small containers of which the largest surface has an area of less than 10 cm2, the information required under this paragraph must be permanently and visibly displayed

requirements.requirement_summary: Labels of foods fortified with vitamins and minerals must not state or imply that a balanced diet cannot provide appropriate quantities of nutrients. They must not mislead consumers about the nutritional merit resulting from the addition. Mandatory nutrition labelling including total amounts of added vitamins and minerals is required.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators placing foods with added vitamins and minerals on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies to foods to which vitamins and minerals have been added under Regulation (EC) No 1925/2006
requirements.source_text_snippet: Nutrition labelling of products to which vitamins and minerals have been added and which are covered by this Regulation shall be compulsory

requirements.requirement_summary: Member States may require manufacturers or persons placing fortified foods on the market to notify the competent authority of that placing on the market by providing a model of the label used for the product.
requirements.applicable_to: manufacturers or persons placing fortified foods or foods with Annex III substances on the market
requirements.conditions: Applies only where a Member State has adopted a notification requirement for fortified foods
requirements.source_text_snippet: Member States may require the manufacturer or the person placing such foods on the market in their territory to notify the competent authority of that placing on the market

requirements.article_title: Notification of a traditional food from a third country
requirements.requirement_summary: An applicant intending to place a traditional food from a third country on the EU market may submit a notification to the Commission including: name/address, food name/description, detailed composition, country of origin, documented safe use history, and a proposal for labelling requirements that do not mislead consumers.
requirements.applicable_to: applicants intending to place traditional foods from third countries on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies only to traditional foods from third countries as an alternative to the standard authorisation procedure in Article 10
requirements.source_text_snippet: an applicant, who intends to place on the market within the Union a traditional food from a third country, may opt to submit a notification of that intention to the Commission

requirements.requirement_summary: Any food business operator that has placed a novel food on the market must immediately inform the Commission of any new scientific or technical information that might affect the safety evaluation, or any prohibition or restriction imposed by a third country where the novel food is marketed.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators that have placed a novel food on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies only to novel foods already placed on the market
requirements.source_text_snippet: Any food business operator which has placed a novel food on the market shall immediately inform the Commission of any information of which it has become aware

requirements.requirement_summary: Products of animal origin handled in approved establishments must not be placed on the market without either a health mark (per Regulation (EC) No 854/2004) or an identification mark (per Annex II, Section I of Regulation (EC) No 853/2004). An identification mark may only be applied if the product was manufactured in compliance with the regulation.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators handling products of animal origin in approved establishments
requirements.conditions: Applies to products of animal origin handled in establishments subject to approval under Article 4(2)
requirements.source_text_snippet: Food business operators shall not place on the market a product of animal origin handled in an establishment subject to approval... unless it has either a health mark... or an identification mark

requirements.requirement_summary: Imported food must comply with the hygiene requirements laid down in Articles 3 to 6 of Regulation (EC) No 852/2004, as part of the relevant food law requirements referenced in Article 11 of Regulation (EC) No 178/2002.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators importing food into the EU
requirements.conditions: Applies to food imported into the EU
requirements.source_text_snippet: the relevant requirements of food law referred to in Article 11 of Regulation (EC) No 178/2002 shall include the requirements laid down in Articles 3 to 6 of this Regulation

requirements.requirement_summary: Food must not be placed on the market if it is unsafe — meaning injurious to health or unfit for human consumption. Safety assessment must consider normal conditions of use, consumer information, probable health effects (immediate, short-term, long-term, cumulative), and sensitivities of specific consumer categories.
requirements.applicable_to: all food business operators placing food on the EU market
requirements.source_text_snippet: Food shall not be placed on the market if it is unsafe

requirements.article_title: Traceability requirements for products for food and feed produced from GMOs
requirements.requirement_summary: When placing products produced from GMOs on the market, operators must transmit in writing to the receiving operator an indication of each food ingredient produced from GMOs, each feed material/additive produced from GMOs, or (where no ingredients list exists) an indication that the product is produced from GMOs. Operators must maintain systems to hold this information and identify trading partners for five years.
requirements.applicable_to: operators placing products produced from GMOs on the market
requirements.conditions: Applies to products produced from GMOs; does not apply to traces below established thresholds that are adventitious or technically unavoidable
requirements.source_text_snippet: operators shall ensure that the following information is transmitted in writing to the operator receiving the product

requirements.article_title: Traceability and labelling requirements for products consisting of or containing GMOs
requirements.requirement_summary: Pre-packaged products consisting of or containing GMOs must bear a label stating 'This product contains genetically modified organisms' or 'This product contains genetically modified [name of organism(s)]'. For non-pre-packaged products, the same words must appear on or in connection with the product display.
requirements.applicable_to: operators placing products consisting of or containing GMOs on the EU market
requirements.conditions: Applies to products consisting of or containing GMOs; does not apply to traces below established thresholds that are adventitious or technically unavoidable
requirements.source_text_snippet: for pre-packaged products consisting of, or containing GMOs, the words 'This product contains genetically modified organisms' or 'This product contains genetically modified [name of organism(s)]' appear on a label

requirements.requirement_summary: Exported or re-exported food must comply with the hygiene requirements laid down in Articles 3 to 6 of Regulation (EC) No 852/2004.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators exporting or re-exporting food from the EU
requirements.conditions: Applies to food being exported or re-exported from the EU
requirements.source_text_snippet: the relevant requirements of food law referred to in Article 12 of Regulation (EC) No 178/2002 shall include the requirements laid down in Articles 3 to 6 of this Regulation

requirements.requirement_summary: Food business operators handling products of animal origin must comply with the relevant provisions of Annexes II and III of Regulation (EC) No 853/2004. Only potable water or clean water (where permitted) may be used to remove surface contamination from products of animal origin, unless the substance has been specifically approved by the Commission.
requirements.applicable_to: food business operators handling products of animal origin
requirements.conditions: Applies to food business operators handling products of animal origin
requirements.source_text_snippet: Food business operators shall not use any substance other than potable water or, when Regulation (EC) No 852/2004 or this Regulation permits its use, clean water, to remove surface contamination from products of animal origin
